source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: Current models of electron scattering do not account for chemical bonding effects
We propose a Bayesian approach to estimate atomic scattering factors from cryo-EM maps
Empirical scattering factors show improved agreement with cryo-EM data
The method decreases variance of unmodeled signal by up to a factor of three
Improved scattering factors may enable more accurate determination of atomic displacement parameters
Further improvements require higher-resolution structures with less radiation damage

typical_pattern: Explain the technical basis or rationale

likely_priorities: Accuracy of scattering factors near s=0
Quality and resolution of experimental data
Proper treatment of hydrogen atom coordinates
Separation of charge effects from radiation damage effects
likely_attacks: Hydrogen atom coordinates not individually refined but extrapolated
Scattering factors near s=0 currently limited in accuracy
Trend in low-frequency scattering may still be influenced by charges and radiation damage
No modeling of solvent contribution to the electrostatic potential
Assumption that atoms are spherical is problematic for hydrogen
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The discussion explicitly acknowledges multiple limitations: hydrogen coordinates have not been individually refined but extrapolated from nonhydrogen positions; accuracy of scattering factors near s=0 is currently limited; the trend in low-frequency scattering may still be influenced by negative charges on oxygens, positive charges on nitrogens, and radiation damage; no attempt has been made to model the solvent contribution to the ESP; the assumption that atoms are spherical is problematic for hydrogen. The authors also state there is a need for higher-resolution structures with much less radiation damage and suggest a larger experimental dataset possibly ten times larger than typical.

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, invented numbers, or author names were included. The profile is based solely on the provided discussion, abstract, and figure legend content.
